title: facility — TenancyHook + lifecycle hooks
description: Cross-module hook implementations. TenancyHooks are the tenancy module's contract for reacting to tenant-lifecycle events (provision / suspend / erase). Facility does not register a bootstrapper (nothing to hydrate at tenant-boot), but registers three cascade hooks: branch-archived prevention, facility-retired cascade, membership-terminated cascade (Wave 4 placeholder).

hooks.name: PreventFacilityOrphansOnBranchArchived
hooks.purpose: Refuse the archive when any facility referencing the branch still has status='active'. Distinct from the FK CASCADE — we deliberately BLOCK because facility retirement is an ops decision, not a side-effect.
hooks.returns: BRANCH_HAS_ACTIVE_FACILITIES (409) with the facility count in the error payload

hooks.name: CancelFutureBookingsOnFacilityRetired
hooks.purpose: Cascades: cancels every future booking against the retired facility. Fires BookingCancelled per booking with cascade_trigger='facility_retired'. Runs at priority 50 (before notification listeners) so the notification fan-out sees the cancelled state.
hooks.note: Duplicated in listeners.json with the same class — hooks + listeners are two views of the same registration for policy compliance

hooks.name: CascadePassesOnMembershipTerminated
hooks.purpose: Wave 4 bridge. Revokes every Pass tied to the terminated Membership. Wave 2 ships the class as a placeholder — event doesn't exist yet.

hooks.name: PurgeFacilityDataForErasedTenant
hooks.purpose: Cascade delete via FK; writes compliance audit trail entry + emits count metrics per entity family (facilities / resource_bookings / day_passes / passes purged). Audit rows outlive the source rows per financial retention.
hooks.compliance: GDPR Art. 17 (right to erasure). Emitted metrics feed the compliance dashboard for retention proof.

no_bootstrapper.reason: Facility does not need a tenant-boot hydration path — everything is on-demand load from the DB. Contrast with modules like `tenancy` (bootstraps request-context) or `settings` (bootstraps config cache) that need per-request tenant hydration.
